canonical_title: Future of Life Institute AI Safety Index says major AI companies weakened safety pledges
aliases: AI Safety Index safety pledges retreat
Future of Life Institute AI Safety Index 2026
AI companies retreat from safety pledges
FLI AI Safety Index Anthropic OpenAI Google DeepMind Meta
treatment: Treat as a safety-governance report, not as a model release. Reinclude only if FLI publishes materially revised scores, a named lab issues a substantive response or policy change, regulators cite the report, or new evidence changes interpretation of voluntary safety frameworks.
known_facts: Axios reported on 2026-07-07 that the Future of Life Institute's latest AI Safety Index says major AI companies have weakened key safety commitments even as capabilities grow.
Axios reported that Anthropic ranked first but received only C+ overall, while OpenAI and Google DeepMind received C grades.
The report says Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind and Meta weakened or eliminated earlier commitments to pause development if systems approached specified danger thresholds.
Axios noted that existential safety was the weakest category across the industry and that Mistral criticized the index as penalizing open-weight approaches.
reinclude_only_if: FLI releases a materially revised AI Safety Index scorecard or methodology.
Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta, Mistral, xAI, DeepSeek, or another evaluated lab publishes a substantive response or policy change.
A regulator, standards body, or government proceeding cites the report as evidence for a concrete policy action.
New evidence appears about removal or restoration of pause-threshold commitments, model release gates, or existential-safety controls.
notes: Added from LLM Night Lite after the 2026-07-07 midday brief. Primary source located via Axios report; official FLI page was not retrieved in this run, so confidence remains below high.
